verses.text: David rebukes the boastful tyrant who glories in mischief, his tongue a sharp razor loving lies more than truth. Cruel power vaunts itself. But the man who makes evil his strength is already condemned.

David rebukes the boastful tyrant who glories in mischief, his tongue a sharp razor loving lies more than truth. Cruel power vaunts itself. But the man who makes evil his strength is already condemned.

David rebukes the boastful tyrant who glories in mischief, his tongue a sharp razor loving lies more than truth. Cruel power vaunts itself. But the man who makes evil his strength is already condemned.

David rebukes the boastful tyrant who glories in mischief, his tongue a sharp razor loving lies more than truth. Cruel power vaunts itself. But the man who makes evil his strength is already condemned.

God will pluck him out and uproot him for ever; the righteous shall see, fear, and say, 'Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength.' His fall becomes a sermon. To trust in riches and wickedness instead of God ends in ruin.

God will pluck him out and uproot him for ever; the righteous shall see, fear, and say, 'Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength.' His fall becomes a sermon. To trust in riches and wickedness instead of God ends in ruin.

God will pluck him out and uproot him for ever; the righteous shall see, fear, and say, 'Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength.' His fall becomes a sermon. To trust in riches and wickedness instead of God ends in ruin.

'But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God,' David says, trusting His mercy for ever and praising Him. Rooted in God, he flourishes. The godly, planted near God, outlast the proud who scorn Him.
